titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Ralph Emerson Papers
titlestmt.author: Andover Newton Theological School and Yale Divinity Library staff

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 22:21

did.abstract: The Ralph Emerson Papers (1807-1871) primarily document the professional work of Emerson as a Congregational clergyman and faculty member at Andover Theological Seminary. Letters to and from his wife Eliza are included in the correspondence.

chronitemset.event: Minister, First Congregational Church, Norfolk, CT
Brown Professor of Ecclesiastical History, Andover Theological Seminary
Minister, South Congregational Church, Andover, MA
President of the Faculty, Andover Theological Seminary

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from Andover Newton Theological School Collection, 2017

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Ralph Emerson Papers (1807-1871) primarily document the professional work of Emerson. The collection is arranged according to the following categories: journal, correspondence, student lecture notes and essays from Yale College and Andover Theological Seminary, lectures, essays, addresses, sermons, publications, and notes on readings. Included in the correspondence are letters to and from his wife, Eliza.

The majority of sermons are in a shorthand system probably devised by Emerson. There is no extant key to the system, although it might be possible to compile a key by analyzing a letter of Emerson to Charles Prentice, November 7, 1829, of which there are two, one in shorthand and one in longhand.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series III. Notes, Lectures and Writings

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Ralph Emerson Papers (RG 285), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (in Daybook I, student papers, Andover Theological Seminary, p. 10)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (in Daybook II, student papers, Andover Theological Seminary, p. 37)

did.unittitle: Reminiscences of myself and others (autobiographical essay)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a complete list of letters, see the original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

did.unittitle: Undated correspondence, primarily to Eliza Emerson from Charlotte and Mary Rockwell
Letters to / from Ralph Emerson and Eliza Emerson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Rockwell family, Pierce family, Leonard Woods, B. Wisner, First Congregational Church, Norfolk, CT.

did.unittitle: Letters primarily to Ralph Emerson and Eliza Emerson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Rockwell family, Pierce family, and others.

did.unittitle: Letters primarily to Ralph Emerson and Eliza Emerson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Rockwell family, Pierce family, and others.

did.unittitle: Association: Letters: Charles Rockwell to Mrs. Charles Rockwell; from Pontiac, MI (May 18, 1846) --- to D. P. Colburn; from ---

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a full list of titles, see original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including "Chemical lectures", "Hebrew Grammar", and lectures by Dr. Timothy Dwight and Professor George Edward Day

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include "On profanity", "Comparison between a spendthrift and a miser", and essays on God, on Yale students, on human nature

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Including notes on sermons preached at Yale

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All but #9 have sustained fire damage. Including notes on lectures by Professors Stuart, Woods, Park

did.unittitle: Dissertation: The greatness of the preach[er]'s task. (for examination in the department of sacred rhetoric)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fire damaged

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include "Epitome of history", "Evils against which a pastor & his people should especially guard after a season of awakening", "General report presented to the Peace Society in the Theological Seminary, Andover, at their annual meeting, Aug. 1832", "Jerome's account of monkery in Egypt"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Titles include "Valedictory oration on energy of character", "Ecclesiastical history as an element in female education"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For a full list of sermon texts, see the original finding aid, which is linked to from https://web.library.yale.edu/divinity/special-collections/andover-newton.

did.unittitle: Fragments and undated sermons arranged by text
Can there be a wordless thought? (for the New York Observer)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (first draft prepared at the request of Dr. Sprague for insertion in his Annals of the American Pulpit, of which only an abstract was published)

did.unittitle: History of the rise of the Protestant system
Obituary notice of the Hon. William B. Bannister of Newburyport, Mass. for the Congregationalist
A plea for the unmutilated version of the Bible as made by King James' translators
Review of Jesse's History of the House of Stuart
Review of Palfrey's History of New England, volume 1 (2 folders)
On the origin and commencement of the Reformation; from Planck's Protestant theology. American Biblical Repository 9 (1837): 332-358; 10 (1837): 104-141, 253-297

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: translated by Emerson; (proof sheets with some corrections in RE's hand)

did.unittitle: On provoking our children to wrath. The Mother's Magazine and Family Library 11 (1843): 183-189, 193-202 (MS)
The early history of monasticism; from the original sources. Bibliotheca Sacra 1 (1844): 309-331, 464-525, 632-699

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: (4 folders--proof sheets, with corrections in RE's hand)

did.unittitle: Early history of monkery in the church--from original sources. (rough draft of above)
Wiggers, G.F. An historical presentation of Augustinism and Pelagianism from the original sources. Trans. Ralph Emerson. Andover: Gould, Newman & Saxon, 1840
Life of Rev. Joseph Emerson, pastor of the Third Congregational Beverly, Ms. and subsequently principal of a Female Seminary, Boston: Crocker and Brewster, 1833.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Incomplete manuscript

did.unittitle: Proof sheets of articles by Emerson [?]: Church history, Review of the works of Nathanael Emmons, D.D.
Notes on reading arranged by title
